en.extract:
The rufous-browed flycatcher (Anthipes solitaris) is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae.  It is native to Indonesia, Laos, Malaysia, Myanmar, Thailand and Vietnam. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forests. It was formerly placed in the genus Ficedula.

The '''rufous-browed flycatcher''' ('''''Anthipes solitaris''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Muscicapidae]].  It is native to [[Indonesia]], [[Laos]], [[Malaysia]], [[Myanmar]], [[Thailand]] and [[Vietnam]]. Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s. It was formerly placed in the genus ''[[Ficedula]]''.<ref name="iucn status 15 November 2021" />
